purpose: Defines the data contract for Product Owner review artifacts. A review artifact records how a human Product Owner is evaluating proposed recommendation candidates from a recommendation set (for example rec-002), without itself changing the deck, without editing the candidate records it reviews, and without creating a decision log entry. Review is a separate layer that sits between recommendation candidates (generated, non-actionable) and decision logs (human-authorized deck changes).

includes: The review artifact contract: required top-level fields and required review-entry fields.
Allowed review_status values and their meaning.
The explicit distinction between accepted_for_decision and an actual deck change.
Boundary rules describing what a review artifact can never do.
Traceability requirements linking every review entry back to its recommendation set and candidate.
excludes: Accepting, rejecting, or deferring any candidate.
Modifying recommendation candidate records (rec-001, rec-002, or any future rec-NNN).
Creating or modifying decision log entries.
Creating or modifying deck version files.

explicit_no_deck_change_boundary.statement: Review artifacts record Product Owner review intent only. No review artifact, by itself, accepts, rejects, defers, authorizes, or implements a deck change. A deck change requires a decision log entry and a new deck version file, both created in a later, separate task.

accepted_for_decision.meaning: accepted_for_decision means the Product Owner has decided this candidate may proceed to a future decision log task. It is a review-layer signal only.
deck_change_requires: A new deck version file in workshop/projects/the-myr-singularity/versions/.

rejected.meaning: rejected means the Product Owner does not want this candidate to proceed further. It does not delete or alter the candidate record itself.

deferred.meaning: deferred means review is paused pending more information, time, or priority, and may return to under_review later.

needs_testing.meaning: needs_testing means the Product Owner wants playtest or simulation evidence before continuing review.

under_review.meaning: under_review is the initial, neutral state: the Product Owner has not yet formed a review conclusion.

review_entry_schema.description: Contract for each entry of a review artifact's 'review_entries' array.

candidate_id.description: The candidate_id being reviewed, matching a candidate_id in the referenced recommendation set exactly, for example 'cand-007'.

recommendation_set_id.description: The recommendation_set_id the candidate belongs to, for example 'rec-002'. Every review entry must remain traceable to its recommendation set.

review_status.description: The Product Owner's current review status for this candidate. New review entries start as 'under_review'.

reviewer_role.description: The role of the person recording this review, for example 'Product Owner'.

reviewer_notes.description: Free-text reviewer notes. May be empty until the Product Owner writes notes. Neutral language only.

rationale.description: The Product Owner's stated reasoning for the review_status. May be empty until a review conclusion is formed.

testing_required.description: Whether this candidate needs playtest or simulation evidence before it can be accepted for decision. Null or unresolved until the Product Owner assesses it.

testing_notes.description: Notes about what testing is needed or has been observed. May be empty.

decision_log_required.description: True when this candidate, if ever accepted, must be recorded in a decision log entry before any further action. Mirrors the candidate's own decision_log_required field.

creates_new_deck_version_if_accepted.description: True when accepting this candidate would eventually require a new deck version file. Mirrors the candidate's own creates_new_deck_version field.

reviewed_at.description: ISO 8601 date the review_status was last set by the Product Owner. Null until a real review conclusion is recorded.

boundary_rules: Review entries cannot modify candidate records. The recommendation file being reviewed (for example rec-002.json) is never edited by a review artifact.
Review entries cannot modify deck versions. No versions/*.json file is created or edited by a review artifact.
Review entries cannot create decisions by themselves. A decision log entry is a separate, later artifact created only after Product Owner acceptance.
Review entries cannot promote candidate facts into deck facts. workshop/card-data/cards.json and workshop/card-data/candidate_cards.json are never edited by a review artifact.
Every candidate must remain traceable to its recommendation set: every review entry's recommendation_set_id and candidate_id must resolve to a real candidate in that recommendation set's JSON file.
validation_expectations: The review artifact JSON parses as JSON and has every required top-level field.
source_recommendation_file points to an existing, parseable recommendation JSON file.
Every review_entries candidate_id exists in the referenced recommendation set.
Every candidate in the referenced recommendation set has exactly one review entry.
No review entry references a candidate_id absent from the recommendation set.
Newly scaffolded review entries have review_status 'under_review', reviewed_at null, and are not accepted_for_decision, rejected, deferred, or needs_testing.
decision_log_required and creates_new_deck_version_if_accepted mirror the candidate's own boundary fields for candidates that could lead to deck changes.
The review artifact's explicit_boundary states that no deck change is authorized.
The review markdown companion states the Product Owner review purpose, the no-deck-change boundary, and mentions every candidate ID it covers.
